curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 11 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of combination, atomic theory
Apply mole concept, molar masses, percentage composition, stoichiometry calculations
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels
Trace discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution from Bohr's model to Quantum Mechanical Model
Apply modern periodic law and understand genesis of periodic classification
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, VSEPR theory, Valence Bond Theory, Hybridisation, and Molecular Orbital Theory
Apply concepts of calorimetry, reaction enthalpies, spontaneity, and equilibrium via Gibbs Energy
Apply Law of Chemical Equilibrium, understand homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, relationship between K, Q, G
Apply classical ideas, oxidation numbers, and electrode processes
Apply structural representations, nomenclature, isomerism concepts, and fundamental reaction mechanism principles
Classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic hydrocarbons

units.unitName: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
units.description: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
topics: Modern Periodic Law and Table Structure
Electronic Configuration and Block Types (s, p, d, f)
Law of Chemical Equilibrium and Kc/Kp
Relation between Q, K, and Gibbs Energy
Classical Idea vs Electron Transfer View

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks

internal_assessment.note: Schools can integrate formative assessment topics (e.g., s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State for Class XI; Surface Chemistry, General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements, Polymers, Chemistry in Everyday life for Class XII) without adding to summative assessments.

sections: A. Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting glass tube/rod, Bending a glass tube, Drawing out a glass jet, Boring a cork)
B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Determination of melting point, boiling point, Crystallization of Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid)
C. Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH using paper/indicator, Comparing strong vs weak acids, Study pH change in titration/common-ion effect)
D. Chemical Equilibrium (Shift between ferric ions/thiocyanate or [Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions)
E. Quantitative Estimation (Using balance, Preparation of standard Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate, Titration for strength determination)
F. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt; Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds)
A. Surface Chemistry (Preparation of lyophilic/lyophobic sol, Dialysis, Study role of emulsifying agents)
B. Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on reaction rate between Sodium Thiosulphate and HCl; Reaction rates study using Iodide/Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate/Sodium Sulphite)
C. Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution, Enthalpy of neutralization, Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform)
D. Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration changes)
E. Chromatography (Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers, Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture)
F. Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate/Potash Alum; Potassium Ferric Oxalate)
G. Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide/Di-benzal acetone/p-Nitroacetanilide/Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye)
H. Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino groups)
I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
J. Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
K. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation; Detection of Nitrogen/Sulphur/Chlorine)
mandatory_experiments: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Ferrous ammonium sulphate

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness/presence of Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in regional drinking water
Investigation of foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Study of presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect)
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of rate of fermentation (wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants in fat/oil/butter/sugar/turmeric/chili powder/pepper

general_requirements: Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with teacher approval.
Students are expected to record at least five listed experiments in practical file for visually challenged students.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks

internal_assessment.note: Schools can integrate formative assessment topics (e.g., s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State for Class XI; Surface Chemistry, General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements, Polymers, Chemistry in Everyday life for Class XII) without adding to summative assessments.

sections: A. Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting glass tube/rod, Bending a glass tube, Drawing out a glass jet, Boring a cork)
B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Determination of melting point, boiling point, Crystallization of Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid)
C. Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH using paper/indicator, Comparing strong vs weak acids, Study pH change in titration/common-ion effect)
D. Chemical Equilibrium (Shift between ferric ions/thiocyanate or [Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions)
E. Quantitative Estimation (Using balance, Preparation of standard Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate, Titration for strength determination)
F. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt; Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds)
A. Surface Chemistry (Preparation of lyophilic/lyophobic sol, Dialysis, Study role of emulsifying agents)
B. Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on reaction rate between Sodium Thiosulphate and HCl; Reaction rates study using Iodide/Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate/Sodium Sulphite)
C. Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution, Enthalpy of neutralization, Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform)
D. Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration changes)
E. Chromatography (Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers, Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture)
F. Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate/Potash Alum; Potassium Ferric Oxalate)
G. Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide/Di-benzal acetone/p-Nitroacetanilide/Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye)
H. Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino groups)
I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
J. Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
K. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation; Detection of Nitrogen/Sulphur/Chlorine)
mandatory_experiments: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Ferrous ammonium sulphate

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness/presence of Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in regional drinking water
Investigation of foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Study of presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect)
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of rate of fermentation (wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants in fat/oil/butter/sugar/turmeric/chili powder/pepper

general_requirements: Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods of work can be chosen with teacher approval.
Students are expected to record at least five listed experiments in practical file for visually challenged students.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, enable representation at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and inculcate problem-solving skills.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of combination, atomic theory
Apply mole concept, molar masses, percentage composition, stoichiometry calculations
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns underlying chemical phenomena through understanding atomic models, quantum mechanics, and electronic configurations.
competencies: Trace discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution from Bohr's model to Quantum Mechanical Model

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in element properties through classification, periodic law, electronic configuration, and trends across blocks.
competencies: Apply modern periodic law and understand genesis of periodic classification
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent chemical phenomena at molecular levels using various bonding theories.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach, VSEPR theory, Valence Bond Theory, Hybridisation, and Molecular Orbital Theory

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply thermodynamic principles including enthalpy, entropy, Gibbs energy, and spontaneity.
competencies: Apply concepts of calorimetry, reaction enthalpies, spontaneity, and equilibrium via Gibbs Energy

competency_groups.title: Chemical Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in equilibrium states and factors affecting them.
competencies: Apply Law of Chemical Equilibrium, understand homogeneous/heterogeneous equilibria, relationship between K, Q, G

competency_groups.title: Redox and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent redox phenomena in terms of electron transfer.
competencies: Apply classical ideas, oxidation numbers, and electrode processes

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds structurally and understand reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Apply structural representations, nomenclature, isomerism concepts, and fundamental reaction mechanism principles
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons and understand their properties.
competencies: Classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic hydrocarbons

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply concepts of solution concentration, solubility, and colligative properties.
competencies: Apply expressions for concentration, ideal/non-ideal solutions, vapor pressure, and determination of molar mass

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry Applications
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply electrochemical principles including cells, Nernst equation, and corrosion.
competencies: Apply concepts of galvanic/electrolytic cells, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics Analysis
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze reaction rates and factors influencing them.
competencies: Apply integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, collision theory

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in transition elements, lanthanoids, and actinoids.
competencies: Apply electronic configurations, general properties of d-block, applications

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds Bonding
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent coordination compounds using Werner's theory, bonding models, and isomerism.
competencies: Apply nomenclature, isomerism concepts, metal carbonyl bonding

competency_groups.title: Organic Functional Groups and Synthesis
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds with functional groups, understand nomenclature, preparation methods, and reactions.
competencies: Apply classification, nomenclature, structures of haloalkanes/haloarenes, alcohols/phenols/ethers

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Chemistry and Amines
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent carbonyl groups, carboxyl groups, and amines structurally.
competencies: Apply nomenclature of aldehydes/ketones/carboxylic acids/amines

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Structure and Function
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent biomolecules including carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, nucleic acids.
competencies: Represent structures of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, hormones

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in colloidal states, adsorption, emulsions.
competencies: Apply concepts of physisorption/chemisorption, lyophilic/lyophobic properties

competency_groups.title: Isolation and Industrial Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply principles of extraction, concentration, oxidation, reduction.
competencies: Apply methods of isolation including electrolytic refining
competency_groups.unit_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
competency_groups.chapter_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

competency_groups.title: Polymers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent polymer structures including homopolymers and copolymers.
competencies: Apply concepts of polymerisation

competency_groups.title: Chemistry in Everyday Life and Environment
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to contribute to frontier research areas related to climate change, environmental issues.
competencies: Apply knowledge of chemicals in medicines (analgesics, antibiotics), food preservatives

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks

internal_assessment.note: Schools can integrate formative assessment topics (e.g., s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State for Class XI; Surface Chemistry, General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements, Polymers, Chemistry in Everyday life for Class XII) without adding to summative assessments.

sections: A. Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting glass tube/rod, Bending a glass tube, Drawing out a glass jet, Boring a cork)
B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Determination of melting point, boiling point, Crystallization of Alum/Copper Sulphate/Benzoic Acid)
C. Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH using paper/indicator, Comparing strong vs weak acids, Study pH change in titration/common-ion effect)
D. Chemical Equilibrium (Shift between ferric ions/thiocyanate or [Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions)
E. Quantitative Estimation (Using balance, Preparation of standard Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate, Titration for strength determination)
F. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt; Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds)
A. Surface Chemistry (Preparation of lyophilic/lyophobic sol, Dialysis, Study role of emulsifying agents)
B. Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on reaction rate between Sodium Thiosulphate and HCl; Reaction rates study using Iodide/Hydrogen Peroxide or Potassium Iodate/Sodium Sulphite)
C. Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution, Enthalpy of neutralization, Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform)
D. Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration changes)
E. Chromatography (Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers, Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture)
F. Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate/Potash Alum; Potassium Ferric Oxalate)
G. Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide/Di-benzal acetone/p-Nitroacetanilide/Aniline yellow or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye)
H. Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino groups)
I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
J. Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
K. Qualitative Analysis (Determination of one anion and one cation; Detection of Nitrogen/Sulphur/Chlorine)
mandatory_experiments: Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Ferrous ammonium sulphate
